meta.title: Publishing Guide | AKT Research Foundation
meta.description: Submit research and follow the foundation's publishing guidance.

header.title: Submit Research & Publishing Guide
header.subtitle: A concise academic guide to preparing, submitting, and publishing with the foundation.

overview.title: Overview
overview.body: The AKT Research Foundation publishes scholarship that advances historical, cultural, and interdisciplinary research. We welcome rigorous work grounded in primary sources and transparent methodology.

eligibility.title: Eligibility & Scopes
items: Open to journal articles, critical editions, and curated collections.
Accepted languages include Arabic, English, French, and Spanish.
We consider archival research, documentary heritage, and cross-disciplinary studies.

guidelines.title: Author Guidelines
items: Provide an abstract, keywords, and a clear research question.
Follow consistent citation style and include a complete bibliography.
Ensure figures and images include captions, sources, and permissions.

policies.title: Policies
items: Submissions must be original and not under review elsewhere.
Disclose conflicts of interest and funding sources.
Data and sources should be accessible when possible; licensing follows CC BY-NC.

items.label: Manuscript Template
items.description: Use this structured template for articles and reports.

items.label: Cover Letter
items.description: Briefly describe the contribution and relevance of your work.

items.q: Do you accept previously published work?
items.a: We consider substantially revised versions with clear added value.

items.q: Can I submit in multiple languages?
items.a: Yes, but each submission should focus on one primary language.
Accepted manuscripts move to editing, copyediting, and final proof.
